description:
Advances: specled.config, specled.mix_tasks, specled.branch_guard

Deliverable:
Expose `.spec/config.yml` `guardrails.severities` override for the 12 new finding codes (10 append_only/* + 2 overlap/*), add a default filter in `mix spec.check` output that suppresses `:info` findings unless `--verbose` or `SPECLED_SHOW_INFO=1` is set. Close C11 on severity ergonomics.

Concrete additions:
- extend `SpecLedEx.Config` to parse `guardrails.severities` as `%{code => severity_atom}`, mirroring the existing `branch_guard.severities` pattern (severity atoms: `:error | :warning | :info | :off`).
- route the new map into `BranchCheck.Severity.resolve/3` (or equivalent) so per-finding-code overrides apply before the trailer override evaluates.
- add a `--verbose` flag to `mix spec.check`. Default output filters out findings with resolved severity `:info`; `--verbose` or `SPECLED_SHOW_INFO=1` un-filters. The state.json findings list always carries every finding regardless of stdout filtering.
- update `.spec/specs/config.spec.md` with a new requirement describing `guardrails.severities` and its precedence.
- update `.spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md` with a new requirement describing the `--verbose` flag and the default `:info` suppression.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: mix test test/specled_ex/config_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs test/specled_ex/severity_integration_test.exs
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - `guardrails.severities.append_only/requirement_deleted: warning` in config.yml downgrades the finding (T7).
  - `mix spec.check` (default) does not print findings with `:info` severity on stdout.
  - `mix spec.check --verbose` prints all findings including `:info`.
  - `SPECLED_SHOW_INFO=1 mix spec.check` also prints `:info` findings.
  - state.json (written by spec.validate) still includes `:info` findings unconditionally.
- New requirements under specled.config and specled.mix_tasks must have executable verification stubs added during this stage.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/append_only.ex (W2a)
- lib/specled_ex/overlap.ex (W2b)
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex core diff path (W2b) - wiring severities through `resolve/3` stays in severity.ex / config.ex surface
- .spec/decisions/* (no new ADRs in this stage)

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/config.ex
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check/severity.ex or equivalent severity-resolution helper
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex
- test/specled_ex/config_test.exs
- test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs (or whichever task-test file covers spec.check)
- test/specled_ex/severity_integration_test.exs (add guardrails cases)
- .spec/specs/config.spec.md (new requirement for guardrails.severities)
- .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md (new requirement for --verbose flag)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not introduce a new environment variable other than `SPECLED_SHOW_INFO`.
- Do not expose `branch_guard.severities` / `guardrails.severities` as a merged key - they are separate maps with separate precedence (guardrails.severities covers only the 12 new codes).
- Do not change default severities for the 12 codes without authoring a new ADR (defaults are locked by specled.decision.append_only_finding_budget).
- Do not mutate the state.json schema - findings still carry their original severity plus resolved severity (existing contract).

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] CLI default vs verbose output test green
- [ ] severity_integration_test.exs covers at least one guardrails.severities override case
- [ ] Spec verification passes for specled.config and specled.mix_tasks additions
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

IMPLEMENTED
Session: dbd2428d-edaf-4363-b1e1-b4699b1b5126
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/dbd2428d-edaf-4363-b1e1-b4699b1b5126.jsonl

Commit: 9ba5276
Files touched:
- lib/specled_ex/config.ex (nested Guardrails struct + parse)
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check/severity.ex (guardrails_severities: opt in resolve/3)
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex (one-line wiring in severity_opts/2 — severity wiring surface, not core diff path)
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex (--verbose flag + SPECLED_SHOW_INFO + filter_for_stdout)
- .spec/specs/config.spec.md (new requirement specled.config.guardrails_severities + scenario + verification)
- .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md (new requirement specled.tasks.check_verbose_flag + verification)
- test/specled_ex/config_test.exs (4 new tests under "load/2 guardrails.severities")
- test/specled_ex/branch_check/severity_integration_test.exs (3 new tests in new describe block for guardrails routing)
- test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs (NEW file, 4 tests for --verbose / env / state.json invariance)
- CHANGELOG.md (two Unreleased entries)

Tests added: SpecLedEx.ConfigTest/guardrails (4), SeverityIntegrationTest/guardrails (3), Mix.Tasks.Spec.CheckTest (4).

Verification stubs flipped / added:
- specled.config verification: added specled.config.guardrails_severities to existing executable command
- specled.mix_tasks verification: added a second executable command targeting test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs covering specled.tasks.check_verbose_flag

Summary: Extends Config with a nested Guardrails struct (parallel to BranchGuard) that parses `guardrails.severities` as a disjoint severity-override namespace for the 12 guardrail codes. Severity.resolve/3 now accepts `guardrails_severities:`; the two config maps collapse to a single config layer for precedence purposes (trailer > config > per_code_default, :off absorbs). mix spec.check gains a --verbose flag and honors SPECLED_SHOW_INFO=1, both gating stdout-only filtering of :info findings; state.json written by spec.validate is unchanged by the flag.

Scope notes for verifier/auditor:
- The single edit to lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex (line 154 adds `guardrails_severities:` to the existing keyword list in severity_opts/2) is the severity-wiring surface the ticket sanctions; the core diff path (contract_findings/5, AppendOnly/Overlap analyzers) is untouched.
- The ticket lists test/specled_ex/severity_integration_test.exs in Allowed touches; the file actually lives at test/specled_ex/branch_check/severity_integration_test.exs. Used the existing file.
- `mix spec.check` on the branch still fails on 33 pre-existing branch findings (down from 34 on baseline — the fm4.5 overlap I initially introduced via two scenarios covering the same requirement was fixed by consolidating into one scenario). All remaining findings pre-existed on this branch and are authoring/ADR debt from fm4.1–fm4.4.
- `mix test`: 527 tests, 0 failures.
- `mix test test/specled_ex/config_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs test/specled_ex/branch_check/severity_integration_test.exs test/specled_ex/branch_check/severity_test.exs`: 52 tests, 0 failures.

title: W3: guardrails.severities config + info-level output filter
